title: gentianopsis detonsa subsp. raupii
intro: Gentianopsis detonsa subsp. raupii, also known as Raup's gentian or Raup's gentianopsis, is a rare and endemic perennial plant species native to the high-altitude mountainous regions of North America, specifically in the western United States and southwestern Canada. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, due to its high concentration of iridoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits associated with Gentianopsis detonsa subsp. raupii include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can potentially help alleviate symptoms of various health conditions, such as digestive issues and respiratory problems. Gentianopsis detonsa subsp. raupii is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its availability and usage may be limited due to its rarity and restricted geographic range.
